intro: Argyreia maymyensis, also known as Maymyo vine, is a climbing plant species in the Convolvulaceae family native to Myanmar. Traditionally, Argyreia maymyensis has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, such as maymyoside, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Argyreia maymyensis is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture in traditional herbal medicine, although its use should be cautious and under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to the lack of extensive scientific research.
